headline: France's traditional parties dream of a return to the classic left-right contest
description: The Socialists and right-wing Les Républicains can boast of successes in the second round of France's municipal elections on March 22. But both parties remain deeply weakened by the lack of a clear direction and the fragmentation of the political landscape, writes Le Monde columnist Françoise Fressoz.

description: The nationalist prime minister is taking advantage of opportunities to rally his foreign supporters, particularly Europeans, in Budapest ahead of the April 12 legislative elections, which could prove difficult for him.

headline: Mette Frederiksen heads into Danish elections as a global figure, but a divisive one domestically
description: The leader of the Social Democrats, in power since 2019, seeks reelection on Tuesday. While her support for Ukraine and her firm stance against Donald Trump regarding Greenland have earned her recognition on the European stage, her party has been struggling in the polls.

headline: US Senate confirms Markwayne Mullin as new chief of Department of Homeland Security
description: Mullin, a Trump ally, replaces Kristi Noem, who was fired earlier this month for her role in the large-scale operation against undocumented migrants in Minnesota.

headline: Five affordable vegetarian restaurants in the Paris region
description: Craving a quinoa arancini, an Asian-inspired salad, or a coconut dessert? Here is our selection of places in Paris and the suburbs where you can enjoy quality vegetarian food for under €20.

headline: Is anti-scooter sentiment fueled by class contempt?
description: Electric scooters are booming in popularity, but local officials are increasingly moving to restrict or ban them. The backlash raises a broader question: Are scooters the future of urban transport or a growing urban irritant?
